zh.lifeSummary: Lady Anne Catherine De Burgh於1478年出生在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾可爾奧爾頓。她於1501年與Sir Thomas Fenton在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾可爾奧爾頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1525年在她的家鄉享年47歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Anne Catherine De Burgh于1478年出生在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾可爾奧爾頓。她于1501年与Sir Thomas Fenton在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾可爾奧爾頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1525年在她的家乡享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Name borne in classical mythology by a queen of Sparta, who was ravished by Zeus in the shape of a swan. She gave birth to two eggs which, when hatched, revealed the two sets of twins: Castor and Pollux, and Helen and Hermione.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
meanings: A name corrpted from Hannah, q.v. It is also found several times in a male name. From Ann, Anne, we have as nicknames Nan and Nancy

variants.name: Nancy
meanings: Of uncertain origin. From the 18th century it is clearly used as a pet form of   Ann   (  see  Nan  ), but it may originally have been a similar formation deriving from the common medieval given name   Annis  , a vernacular form of   Agnes .  Nowadays it is an independent name, and was especially popular in America in the 1930s, 40s, and 50s.

variants.name: Nan
meanings: Originally a pet form of Ann (for the initial N-, compare Ned ). It is now also widely used as a short form of Nancy .

nameMeanings.name: Catherine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Katherine .  This form of the name is also used in France.

variants.name: Catheryn
meanings: Variant spelling of   Katherine .  This form of the name is also used in France.

variants.name: Katherine
meanings: Greek: Same as Catherine.

variants.name: Catharin
meanings: Variant spelling of   Katherine .  This form of the name is also used in France.

variants.name: Cathrine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Katherine .  This form of the name is also used in France.

variants.name: Cathryn
meanings: Variant spelling of   Katherine .  This form of the name is also used in France.

variants.name: Cathrin
meanings: Variant spelling of   Katherine .  This form of the name is also used in France.

variants.name: Catharine
meanings: Greek: Pure.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Anne Catherine De Burgh was born in 1478, in Coleorton, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom. She married Sir Thomas Fenton in 1501, in Coleorton, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1525, in her hometown, at the age of 47.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Anne Catherine De Burgh est née en 1478, à Coleorton, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Fenton en 1501, à Coleorton, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1525, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 47 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Anne Catherine De Burgh wurde 1478 in Coleorton, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat 1501 in Coleorton, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom Sir Thomas Fenton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1525 im Alter von 47 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Anne Catherine De Burgh è nata il nel 1478 nel Coleorton, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Fenton nel 1501, nel Coleorton, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1525, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 47 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Anne Catherine De Burgh(은)는 1478년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 콜리르턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1501년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 콜리르턴에서 Sir Thomas Fenton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1525년 그녀의 고향에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Anne Catherine De Burgh родилась в 1478 г. в местности колеортон, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. Она умерла в 1525 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 47 лет.
